Inv days43d▼
Gross margin27.9%-0.3%
Inv value$12.3bn

Demand SensingMulti-Echelon Inventory OptimizationReal-Time Replenishment

-30 bps gross margin

Markdown OptimizationInventory BalancingAssortment Planning

Integrated Business PlanningNetwork OptimizationCost-to-Serve Analytics
WC: $1,230mInv: -10%Margin: +50bps

Where the value lies

Target holds $12.3bn in inventory with declining sales creating $1.2bn+ working capital release opportunity from 10% reduction. Addressing markdown pressure and in-stock reliability through improved demand planning can recover 50 bps of gross margin worth $524m annually based on $104.8bn revenue base.

Supply Chain Traceability & TransparencyRegulatory Compliance ManagementSustainability Risk Analytics

Demand Sensing & ForecastingCommodity Price Risk ManagementMargin Optimization Planning

Opportunity lies in deploying Blue Yonder-enabled end-to-end supply chain visibility to replace collective moratorium monitoring with company-specific farm-level traceability, protecting $154bn revenue base from reputational and regulatory risks while enabling sustainable volume growth to 51m tonnes.

Combined Spain/Portugal manufacturing GDP estimate, April 2025

10-hour power disruption halted production

$2.3m per hour automotive downtime

Capabilities Needed

Business Continuity PlanningRisk-Based Scenario PlanningMulti-Echelon Inventory Optimization

No backup power infrastructure resilience

60% generation loss in 5 seconds

Industries lacked alternative power supply and redundancy, exposing single-source dependency vulnerability with 15 GW capacity drop.

Capabilities Needed

Supply Risk ManagementSupplier Diversification AnalyticsReal-Time Supply Monitoring

Grid voltage instability under renewable penetration

Tens of millions in losses

Voltage fluctuation triggered cascading failures with 60% solar generation creating fragile infrastructure without synchronous backup.

Capabilities Needed

Predictive Demand SensingCapacity PlanningWhat-If Scenario Modeling

Where the value lies

Manufacturing sectors across Spain/Portugal face $50-150m in estimated disruption costs from 10-hour blackout. Immediate value lies in building resilient S&OP frameworks with backup capacity planning, real-time disruption monitoring, and scenario-based contingency planning to prevent future production halts.

Planning Opportunities

Implement integrated business continuity planning with risk-weighted S&OP processes to model power disruption scenarios and alternative sourcing strategies.

Target Operating Model

Platform Opportunities

SEC Form 6-K

Limited public data
  

Key Issues

Cash COGS per hectoliter rising sharply

4.5%-7.5%

Cash COGS per hectoliter expected to increase 4.5%-7.5% in 2026 driven by aluminum commodity costs and portfolio mix pressures.

Capabilities Needed

Cost-to-Serve OptimizationDigital Twin SimulationProcurement Analytics

Logistics VP replacement signals operational stress

Executive turnover

Paulo André Zagman replaced by Fernando Maffessoni effective August 2026 amid cost and distribution efficiency challenges.

Capabilities Needed

Transportation OptimizationNetwork DesignWarehouse Management

Logistics and distribution cost pressure

BRL 8.0bn

Logistics and distribution expenses totaled BRL 8.0 billion in 9M25, representing critical cost component requiring efficiency gains.

Capabilities Needed

Route OptimizationFleet ManagementLast-Mile Delivery

Where the value lies

Ambev faces 4.5%-7.5% COGS inflation in 2026 with BRL 8.0bn logistics costs requiring immediate optimization. Leadership change in logistics combined with margin expansion ambitions creates $45-60M annual savings opportunity through warehouse automation, TMS route optimization, and AI-driven supply chain digital twin.

Accenture Fulfillment Solutions

🏭 Autonomous Warehousing

Manhattan WMS with autonomous mobile robots can reduce warehouse labor costs 15-20% across Ambev's complex multi-SKU Latin America distribution network.

🚚 Autonomous Transportation

Blue Yonder TMS with dynamic routing can optimize BRL 8.0bn logistics spend through load consolidation and cold-chain efficiency across Latin America operations.

📦 Autonomous Order Mgmt

Manhattan OMS integration with BEES B2B marketplace (70% GMV growth) enables unified order orchestration across 270M hectoliter production capacity.

🤖 Agentic AI & Digital Twin

Agentic AI digital twin simulates COGS scenarios, optimizes network against 45% commodity exposure, and prescribes distribution efficiency to achieve margin expansion targets.

Where the value lies

Crane's Dublin warehouse expansion and Ireland-US trade complexity present a $12-18M autonomous fulfillment opportunity. 🏭 Autonomous Warehousing

🚚 Autonomous Transportation

📦 Autonomous Order Mgmt

🤖 Agentic AI & Digital Twin

Multi-tier RoutingService Level ManagementCapacity Planning

Where the value lies

Evri's 900m annual parcels across 500+ depots with inconsistent service quality creates $120-180m optimization opportunity through unified TMS routing, automated exception management, and AI-driven delivery predictions. Accenture Fulfillment Solutions

🏭 Autonomous Warehousing

Manhattan WMS can consolidate 500+ depot operations with real-time inventory visibility, automated sortation routing, and cross-dock optimization for 900m+ annual parcels.

🚚 Autonomous Transportation

📦 Autonomous Order Mgmt

🤖 Agentic AI & Digital Twin

Autonomous fulfillment across 280+ manufacturing plants and 750+ warehouses directly addresses the €2.5bn cost gap while strengthening resilience against automotive industry contraction pressures.

Bosch operates 750+ warehouses globally with existing SAP EWM but lacks autonomous orchestration to optimize 280+ plant network and close cost gap.

With daily deliveries to 250,000 customers and massive freight spend, autonomous TMS can streamline logistics operations and consolidate shipments to reduce €2.5bn cost gap.

1. Kroger (KR)

Grocery & Food Retail · $150bn

Large Enterprise (>$3B)

HIGH
 

KPIs

Limited public data
  

Key Issues

Margin Compression and COGS Escalation

Gross margin declined to 22.7% from 23.0% YoY; FIFO gross margin down 9 bps driven by supplier price increases, transportation costs, and competitive price investments. Net profit margin at 0.69%, requiring aggressive cost action.

Suppliers seeking price increases while Kroger faces intense competition from Walmart and Costco. CEO Greg Foran stated the company must press harder on supplier negotiations and lean into direct sourcing to afford price investments and close price gaps with competitors.

Capabilities Needed

Strategic SourcingSupplier NegotiationsDirect Sourcing TransformationCategory Management

Direct Sourcing Transformation Underway

Kroger pivoting from intermediaries to direct sourcing for fresh imports, national brands, and private label. CEO announced intensified focus on going direct where historically used intermediaries, representing potential $500M-1bn opportunity.

Management explicitly calling out direct sourcing as priority area with 'level of intensity that reflects the scale of the opportunity.' Hiring for sourcing roles including bakery, produce, and IT services categories. Import capability expansion and new Global Capability Center referenced.

Capabilities Needed

Direct Sourcing StrategySupplier Discovery & OnboardingGlobal SourcingImport/Export Operations

Store Closures and Network Rationalization

60 store closures over 18 months announced June 2026 with $100M impairment charge Q1 2025. Additional 1,000 corporate staff reductions with $47M severance cost. Closures attributed to declining profits and need to reallocate resources to stronger markets.

33 stores already closed as of March 2026, with closures spanning multiple banners (Kroger, Fred Meyer, Harris Teeter, King Soopers, etc.). Failed Albertsons merger intensified need for cost action. CEO emphasized store closures provide modest financial benefit to reinvest.

Capabilities Needed

Network OptimizationStore Operations ProcurementFacilities ManagementClosure Cost Management

Where the value lies

Kroger presents a $1.5-3bn procurement value opportunity driven by CEO mandate to fund competitive price investments through aggressive supplier negotiations, direct sourcing transformation, and GNFR optimization. Urgency is high given 60 store closures, margin compression to 22.7%, and intense Walmart/Costco competition requiring immediate procurement savings to fund 1-2% identical sales growth targets.

Accenture Fulfillment Solutions

Confidence: high

2. Kohl's (KSS)

Apparel & Retail · $18.9bn

Large Enterprise (>$3B)

HIGH
 

KPIs

Limited public data
  

Key Issues

Third-Party Agent Sourcing Inefficiency

Approximately 20% of merchandise ($3.8bn) sourced through third-party purchasing agents creates cost premium and margin erosion. Activist investors flagged this as procurement inefficiency creating 3-5% margin leak on affected purchases.

Kohl's relies on third-party agents for approximately 20% of total merchandise purchases, which activist investors identified as a source of overpayment and sourcing inefficiency. This indirect sourcing model increases COGS and reduces negotiation leverage compared to direct manufacturer relationships.

Capabilities Needed

Direct Sourcing StrategySupplier Relationship ManagementCategory ManagementGlobal Sourcing Excellence

Tariff Impact and Supply Chain Diversification

$190M tariff costs paid on Phase 1 China imports with ongoing exposure to future tariff volatility. Sales down 4.1% in Q1 2026 with comparable sales down 3.9%, partly attributed to inventory adjustments and tariff mitigation efforts.

Kohl's paid approximately $190M in IEEPA tariffs between 2025-2026 and filed claims for $140M in potential refunds. The company is working to diversify sourcing away from China but faces challenges in maintaining cost competitiveness while managing tariff exposure and geopolitical supply chain risk.

Capabilities Needed

Supply Chain Risk AnalyticsCountry Sourcing StrategyTariff Cost ModelingSupplier Diversification Planning

Indirect Spend Fragmentation and Visibility Gaps

Estimated $1.9-2.8bn in indirect/NFR spend (10-15% of revenue) with limited procurement oversight. Industry benchmarks suggest 8-15% savings opportunity ($150-280M) through better indirect spend management.

Like most retailers, Kohl's indirect spending (facilities, IT, marketing, professional services, logistics) is fragmented across business units with limited centralized visibility or control. Activist investors urged assessment of all non-merchandise spend to increase efficiency.

Capabilities Needed

Spend Analytics & VisibilityIndirect Procurement Operating ModelCategory Management (IT, Marketing, Facilities)Supplier Consolidation

Where the value lies

Kohl's faces a critical procurement transformation opportunity worth $265-470M (1.4-2.5% of revenue) driven by eliminating 3rd-party sourcing inefficiencies, optimizing indirect spend, and mitigating tariff exposure. With operating margins of only 2.8-3.4% and activist investor pressure, procurement-led cost optimization is essential to margin recovery and competitive positioning.

Large Enterprise (>$3B)

HIGH
 

KPIs

Limited public data
  

Key Issues

Tariff-Driven Cost Escalation & Geographic Sourcing Concentration

$85-100M net tariff impact in FY2025, representing 15-20% headwind to operating income of $270-320M, with incremental $160M gross tariff costs projected for FY2026

Victoria's Secret faces significant tariff exposure from China sourcing concentration, requiring $70M in mitigation efforts through vendor cost optimization, sourcing diversification to lower-tariff countries, freight mix adjustments (air to ocean), and selective price increases. FY2026 guidance includes estimated net tariff impact of $40M after mitigation.

Capabilities Needed

Geographic sourcing diversification strategyTotal Cost of Ownership (TCO) modelingSupplier network optimizationAdvanced analytics for tariff scenario planning

Supplier Cost & Margin Pressure Management

Operating margin compressed to 2.8% from 4.4% YoY despite solid top-line execution, requiring aggressive vendor negotiations and cost optimization

Despite 8% comparable sales growth in Q4 and strong operational performance, external cost pressures are consuming margin gains. Company needs systematic supplier cost optimization, freight efficiency improvement, and strategic pricing actions to maintain profitability while executing transformation strategy.

Capabilities Needed

Should-cost modeling and cost engineeringStrategic vendor negotiationsFreight and logistics optimizationPrice elasticity modeling

Supply Chain Agility & Inventory Management

Mid-teens percent inventory increase in Q4 due to modal shift from air to ocean freight, creating working capital pressure and inventory risk

Strategic shift from air to ocean freight to offset tariff costs resulted in early inventory ownership and higher stock levels. Company requires improved demand planning, inventory optimization, and agile supply chain capabilities to balance cost reduction with inventory efficiency while managing tariff uncertainty.

Capabilities Needed

Demand-driven planningS&OP optimizationWorking capital managementMulti-echelon inventory optimization

Where the value lies

Victoria's Secret faces urgent need to mitigate $100M+ annual tariff impact while maintaining margin recovery trajectory. Primary value lies in: (1) Rapid geographic sourcing diversification with optimized supplier network ($40-60M savings opportunity), (2) Vendor cost engineering and strategic negotiations ($20-30M), (3) Freight and logistics optimization ($10-15M), (4) Indirect spend rationalization ($15-20M). Combined procurement transformation can deliver $85-125M in total value through spend optimization, supplier risk mitigation, and AI-enabled intelligent procurement.

Limited public data

Supplier Concentration and Geographic Risk Exposure

Freight and logistics costs significantly impacting COGS and margin structure.

Nike is dismantling parts of its DTC-era distribution network, signaling $2bn cost reduction target over three years through supply chain efficiency improvements and procurement enhancements. The company is shifting from fixed distribution assets to 3PL and flexible capacity models. Five footwear contract manufacturers account for 67% of production, creating concentration risk. Vietnam tariffs at 46%, Chinese imports at 55%.

Nike is executing aggressive sourcing rebalancing to mitigate $1bn tariff exposure, but alternative geographies (Vietnam 51%, Indonesia 28%) are also under tariff pressure. The company has increased product prices by $2-10 per item and is negotiating with suppliers and retail partners to share structural cost increases. However, over 50% production from Vietnam creates new single-country dependency.

The combination of Supplier Risk & Resiliency (to navigate Middle East conflict and multi-country dependencies), Spend Optimization (to deliver on $2bn cost reduction target and offset $1bn tariff impact), and AI-Intelligent Procurement (to drive predictive insights across 664 factories and automate tactical work) creates a $600m-1bn annual value opportunity while building strategic resilience for future disruptions.

Large Enterprise (>$3B)

KPIs

Limited public data
  

Key Issues

Semiconductor Supply Chain Instability & Single-Source Dependencies

Production disruptions costing €2-3bn annually in lost output; semiconductor shortages have forced plant capacity reductions and delivery delays affecting 2M+ vehicle sales in Q1 2026

VW reorganized semiconductor procurement to move from Tier 1 indirect sourcing to direct manufacturer relationships with Intel, NXP, Renesas. Joint procurement with Rivian covers 50+ semiconductor categories. Despite progress, ongoing chip volatility continues to constrain production and requires strategic buffer inventory, increasing working capital costs.

Capabilities Needed

Supplier Risk & Resiliency - Dual sourcing strategiesCategory Intelligence - Semiconductor market forecastingControl Tower - Real-time supply visibility across Tier 2/3

Extreme Cost Pressure & Margin Erosion

Operating margin collapsed to 2.8% in 2025 (down from 9%+ pre-pandemic) with Q1 2026 at 4.3% before special items. VW targeting €1bn overhead cost reduction by 2026 and €10bn performance program savings target through 2026. Proposed restructuring includes 50,000+ job cuts and 4 plant closures.

VW faces unprecedented pressure from Chinese EV competition, tariff impacts, and declining China market share. Cost per vehicle remains 15-20% above Chinese competitors. Procurement must deliver €320M+ annual savings while managing inflation in raw materials, energy costs, and supplier price increases.

Capabilities Needed

Spend Optimization - Zero-based category strategiesShould-Cost Modeling - Technical cost breakdownSupplier Negotiation - Price stabilization frameworks

Procurement Operating Model Complexity Across Brands

Fragmented procurement across VW, Audi, Skoda, SEAT, Porsche brands creates 15-20% synergy leakage estimated at €2-3bn. Redundant supplier relationships, inconsistent contract terms, and siloed category management limit economies of scale.

VW initiated Brand Group Core consolidation in January 2026 to merge procurement, development, and production management across volume brands. The group operates 100+ production sites with decentralized purchasing creating maverick spend, limited spend visibility, and suboptimal terms. Digital infrastructure (VGSPS platform, Catena-X integration) incomplete.

Capabilities Needed

Procurement Reinvention - Target Operating Model designCategory Management - Cross-brand governanceDigital Procurement - P2P automation & analytics

Where the value lies

Volkswagen faces a perfect storm of semiconductor supply instability, extreme margin pressure (2.8-4.3% vs. 9%+ historically), and procurement operating model complexity across brands. The €1-2bn savings opportunity lies in spend optimization (3-6% across €50-80bn indirect/tail spend), procurement reinvention (Brand Group Core consolidation capturing 15-20% efficiency gains), supplier risk mitigation (multi-tier visibility preventing €2-3bn disruption costs), and AI-intelligent procurement (SynOps/ATLAS automating demand forecasting, risk monitoring, and P2P processes). Immediate focus: semiconductor category strategy, cross-brand category management, and digital P2P platform.

Limited public data

Material shortages particularly acute for engine maintenance. Industry now facing structural supply chain paradigm with no normalization expected.

Category Management & Strategic SourcingSupplier Relationship ManagementTotal Cost of Ownership ModelingPrice & Cost Analytics

MRO Supply Chain Stranglehold and AOG Risk

Material shortages causing longer turnaround times, reducing fleet availability; engine shop turnaround times 35% higher; airlines paid $3.1bn in additional maintenance costs due to supply chain constraints in 2025; AOG events averaging $150K-$500K per incident

Global MRO market at $136bn in 2025 (up 8%), projected to reach $193bn by 2030. Engine MRO now 53% of total aftermarket (up from 46% in 2024). Lufthansa operating 14 separate ERP systems complicating spend visibility. Shift to vertical integration and in-house maintenance to combat fragility.

Supplier Risk & Resilience ManagementSupply Chain Control TowerDemand Forecasting & Inventory OptimizationAlternative Sourcing Strategy

Strait of Hormuz closure driving jet fuel surge. Lufthansa forced to implement network optimization, fare increases, and efficiency measures. Risk of fuel shortages at hubs beyond June 2026. Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) procurement adding complexity and cost pressure.

Capabilities Needed

Energy Procurement & Hedging StrategyRisk Management & Scenario PlanningNetwork OptimizationSustainable Procurement (SAF)

Where the value lies

Lufthansa faces an existential procurement crisis with €19bn annual spend under pressure from 200% parts inflation and supply chain stranglehold. The combined value opportunity of €3-5bn over 3 years spans: (1) €1.5-2.8bn from spend optimization addressing MRO cost explosion, fuel volatility, and indirect spend fragmentation; (2) €800M-1.2bn from supplier risk mitigation preventing AOG incidents and ensuring supply continuity; (3) €600-900M from AI-intelligent procurement enabling predictive operations. Procurement reinvention from 14-ERP fragmented model to AI-powered strategic function is critical to survival in the new supplier-controlled paradigm.

Value lies in rapid spend optimization (8-12% savings, $400-650M potential) to preserve cash, procurement reinvention to centralize 3 Poland entities and manage crisis, supplier risk platform for multi-tier visibility and continuity, and AI-powered intelligence for predictive risk and cost optimization. Urgency driven by warehouse expansion amid shutdowns and parent liquidity constraints.

Chemicals · $16bn

Large Enterprise (>$3B)

HIGH
 

KPIs

Limited public data
  

Key Issues

Critical Upstream Supply Chain Disruption - MDI Force Majeure

Force majeure declared May 19, 2026 on all North American MDI products due to carbon monoxide and chlorine feed shortages. MDI prices climbed to $2,715/MT (up $50/MT). Production disruptions extending through Q3 2026 affecting €4-5bn annual MDI revenue stream and triggering concurrent price increases by BASF, Huntsman.

Systemic feedstock constraints (CO/chlorine) impacting multiple producers simultaneously. Covestro's Baytown facility affected along with Dow operations. Supply normalization unlikely before late June with BASF's 45-day July turnaround compounding constraints. Petrochemical raw materials (phenol, benzene, propylene) account for 35% of Covestro's €12bn procurement spend.

Capabilities Needed

Supplier Risk ManagementSupply Chain ResilienceAlternative SourcingReal-time Supply Monitoring

High Procurement Complexity with Fragmented Supplier Base

€12bn annual procurement across 15,000 suppliers with limited spend concentration and visibility. Estimated 10,000+ tail-spend SKUs representing €800M in unmanaged spend. Manual processes increase costs by 5-15% according to industry benchmarks, translating to €600M-1.8bn in inefficiency.

Covestro procures petrochemical feedstocks (phenol, benzene, propylene, toluene, acetone) representing 35% of purchasing value plus significant energy, technical goods, and MRO. Complex product specifications across multiple grades and locations. High fragmentation in indirect categories and tail spend with off-contract spending and low process compliance.

Capabilities Needed

Spend Analytics & ClassificationTail Spend ConsolidationSupplier RationalizationProcurement Digitalization

Energy Cost Volatility and Sustainability Transition Risk

Covestro is energy-intensive with heavy gas dependence for production. Energy prices remain elevated above pre-crisis levels despite stabilization in 2024. Company targets 100% renewable energy by 2035 and net-zero Scope 1&2 emissions requiring significant procurement transformation across energy category representing 15-20% of indirect spend (~€500-800M).

Gas is critical as energy source and process gas with no comprehensive short-term substitute. Company procuring renewable energy through PPAs (achieved 100% renewable electricity at 4 US sites in 2025). Upstream GHG emissions from raw material procurement account for majority of Scope 3 emissions. Requires strategic shift from fossil to alternative feedstocks (biomass, recycled materials, CO2).

Capabilities Needed

Energy Procurement StrategySustainability SourcingContract NegotiationAlternative Materials Sourcing

Where the value lies

Immediate value lies in addressing critical MDI supply disruption through supplier risk resilience programs (prevent future €500M+ revenue impact), while capturing €600M-1bn in annual savings through spend optimization across €12bn procurement base, particularly in petrochemical feedstocks (35% of spend), energy transition, and €800M tail spend consolidation. Long-term transformation value through procurement reinvention and AI-intelligent procurement to build predictive, resilient, sustainable procurement function.

1. Koninklijke Philips N.V. (PHG)

KPIs

Limited public data
  

Key Issues

Geopolitical Supply Chain Disruption & Tariff Volatility

Margin compression from tariff impacts despite achieving 12.3% adjusted EBITA margin in 2025. Continued exposure to trade tensions affecting material costs and availability across global medical device supply chain.

Philips disclosed in 2025 annual filing that ongoing geopolitical conflicts including Ukraine war and Middle East tensions, combined with enacted and proposed tariffs, increased supply chain disruption risks. The company's 2026 outlook explicitly includes currently known tariffs but excludes potential new trade actions.

Capabilities Needed

Geopolitical Risk IntelligenceMulti-Tier Supplier VisibilityTrade Compliance AnalyticsRegional Supply Chain Redesign

Semiconductor Component Obsolescence for Medical Systems

Production constraints and extended lead times for medical devices. Medical systems have longer production lifecycles (10-15 years) than semiconductor availability cycles (3-5 years), creating continuous component obsolescence risk and requiring ongoing electronic component rejuvenation.

Philips medical systems stay in production for longer periods than the life cycle of their semiconductors and require continuous rejuvenation of electronic components. Historical semiconductor lead times ballooned from 56-84 days in 2019 to over 365 days during peak shortages, with inventory depleting from 30-60 days to zero.

Capabilities Needed

Component Lifecycle ManagementPredictive Obsolescence AnalyticsDesign for ManufacturabilityAlternative Component Qualification

Extended Supplier Lead Times & Supply Volatility

Inability to meet customer demand and fulfill orders on time, affecting revenue growth potential. Supply shortages contributed to reduced sales forecasts in 2022 (from 5% to 3% growth) and ongoing delivery challenges.

Philips has prioritized building supply chain resilience and identified over 300 alternate component suppliers requiring printed circuit board redesigns. The company is expanding long-term contracts with strategic suppliers but faces continued challenges from geopolitical tensions and labor shortages affecting third-party suppliers.

Capabilities Needed

Supplier Performance ManagementSupply Chain Control TowerDual-Sourcing StrategyDemand-Supply Synchronization

Where the value lies

Primary value creation lies in building supply chain resilience through supplier risk mitigation and procurement reinvention while capturing 4-7% cost savings (EUR 180-350M annually) through spend optimization amid geopolitical volatility and component shortages. AI-intelligent procurement can accelerate time-to-value and provide predictive capabilities for component obsolescence management.

Apparel & Retail · $21bn

Large Enterprise (>$3B)

MEDIUM
 

KPIs

Limited public data
  

Key Issues

Nature-Related Supply Chain Blind Spots Despite Materiality Analyses

WWF's May 2026 Nat 40 Index identifies Kering among luxury brands whose supply chains remain deeply vulnerable despite materiality analyses due to lack of granular location-specific data. Regulatory risk under EU CSRD requirements and reputational exposure to greenwashing allegations. Potential supply disruptions from unmanaged biodiversity dependencies in raw material sourcing worth $3-5bn annually.

Despite achieving 97% raw material traceability and centralizing procurement platforms, Kering's procurement policies rely on vague voluntary certification schemes with little evidence of reducing ecological pressures. The gap between climate commitments (80% of CAC 40 have SBTi targets) and nature-related data creates unpriced material risk in agricultural dependencies for cotton, leather, exotic skins, and regenerative materials.

Capabilities Needed

Granular Location Data SystemsNature-Based Risk AnalyticsMulti-Tier Traceability Beyond Tier 1Ecosystem Restoration Metrics

Fragmented Procurement Operating Model Across Brand Portfolio

Kering's decentralized brand autonomy model (Gucci, Saint Laurent, Bottega Veneta, Alexander McQueen, Balenciaga) creates procurement inefficiencies and limits spend leverage. Estimated 15-25% spend fragmentation across similar categories. Missed savings opportunity of $300-500M annually through lack of category consolidation and supplier rationalization.

While Kering has launched centralized platforms (Gold Platform 2017, internal purchasing platforms for raw materials), each brand maintains high autonomy in sourcing strategy. Only 5% of textile fibres purchased through pre-reserved regenerative materials mechanism. Supplier development programs covering only 29% of partner energy consumption by end of 2025. Limited cross-brand procurement collaboration despite shared supplier base in Italy and Asia.

Capabilities Needed

Category Management ExcellenceCentralized Procurement Operating ModelSpend Analytics & ConsolidationSupplier Rationalization Programs

Tier 2-4 Supplier Visibility Gaps for Nature-Related Dependencies

While Tier 1 supplier visibility is strong (97% traceability claimed), WWF report highlights overwhelming lack of location-specific data beyond first tier. This creates material exposure to water scarcity, soil degradation, biodiversity loss in raw material origins (cotton from India, dyeing in China, assembly across Asia). Estimated 60-75% of nature-related risks sit in Tier 2-4 suppliers that remain undocumented.

Kering works with 225+ startups and has pre-reserved regenerative materials from Tier 4 suppliers, but only 118 tonnes secured (5% of textile fibres). Voluntary certification schemes lack verification of actual ecological impact. Fashion supply chains span multiple continents with complex sub-supplier networks. Without digital product passports and blockchain-enabled traceability, compliance with EU DPP initiative will be challenging.

Capabilities Needed

Multi-Tier Supply Chain MappingBlockchain/Digital Traceability SolutionsSupplier Environmental Data CollectionGeographic Risk Modeling

Where the value lies

Kering faces a strategic paradox: strong sustainability leadership and 97% traceability claims coexist with WWF-identified blind spots in location-specific nature data. The opportunity is threefold: (1) $250-450M savings through spend consolidation across decentralized brands, (2) regulatory risk mitigation by building EU CSRD-compliant nature transition plans with granular Tier 2-4 visibility, and (3) operational transformation via AI-powered procurement to scale traceability and ESG data collection across 3,000-5,000 suppliers.

Physical supply disruption threatens €15.2bn product portfolio.

Hermès sources cashmere from Inner Mongolia, silk from Brazilian Atlantic Forest, exotic skins from Australia/Asia, and leather from European farms. Climate change, water scarcity, and land degradation create material availability risks. Long-standing supplier relationships (21-year average with top 50) may limit agility to pivot sourcing during disruptions.

Digital Procurement Maturity Gaps Limiting Strategic Value Creation

MTN manages $12bn annual procurement spend across 19 countries serving 300+ million subscribers, yet digital process fragmentation and legacy systems reduce spend visibility, slow decision-making, and limit savings realization by an estimated 5-8% of total spend ($600M-$960M)

MTN has implemented eSourcing tools, spend analytics, and governance frameworks, but the appointment of a new executive with a mandate to 'accelerate digital and sustainable procurement practices' signals ongoing transformation gaps. The company operates across 22 countries with complex multi-market sourcing events requiring enhanced transparency and efficiency.

Capabilities Needed

AI-Powered Spend AnalyticsProcurement Process MiningSource-to-Pay AutomationAdvanced Supplier Performance Management

Operating Model Transformation Required for Multi-Market Complexity

MTN's decentralized procurement legacy across Africa and Middle East markets creates inefficiencies in standardization, category management, and supplier leverage. Operating model gaps impact negotiating power and slow procurement cycle times, resulting in estimated 10-15% higher costs on indirect categories

MTN centralized procurement across 22 countries in the 2010s and appointed a CPO, but the company still faces challenges integrating sourcing and supply chain operations. The new executive's mandate explicitly includes 'strengthening the integration of sourcing and supply chain operations' and 'enhancing agility across markets'.

Capabilities Needed

Target Operating Model DesignCategory Management ExcellenceCentralized vs Decentralized BalanceGovernance Framework Implementation

AI and Advanced Analytics Adoption Gaps Hindering Procurement 4.0 Vision

Despite building PX360 neural engine trained on 35,000 sourcing events, MTN needs to scale AI capabilities across tactical procurement to drive 30%+ productivity gains, predictive supplier risk management, and autonomous procurement processes. Scaling challenges could cost $150M-$250M in unrealized efficiency gains annually

MTN has pioneered AI with recommendation engines, predictive price curves, and scenario planning tools achieving 30% sourcing productivity increases. However, the company needs to accelerate adoption of intelligent automation, chatbots for supplier queries, and predictive analytics beyond strategic sourcing to tactical categories and P2P processes.

Capabilities Needed

Generative AI for ProcurementIntelligent Automation & RPAPredictive Risk AnalyticsCognitive Sourcing Agents

Where the value lies

MTN's $12bn procurement transformation presents $1.4B-$2.1B value opportunity through spend optimization (12-18% of addressable spend), AI-intelligent procurement driving 40-50% efficiency gains, and operating model reinvention positioning procurement as strategic growth enabler across Africa's largest telecom network serving 300M+ subscribers.

4. Almarai (2280.SR)

Beverages & FMCG · $4.4bn

Large Enterprise (>$3B)

MEDIUM
 

KPIs

Limited public data
  

Key Issues

Commodity Price Volatility & Feed Cost Inflation

EBIT margin compressed 100bps YoY to 14% (from 15%) despite 7% revenue growth. Corn, soy, and feed costs exhibit >20% YoY volatility with transportation container costs elevated. Feed represents largest COGS component for dairy/poultry operations.

Q1 2026 showed operating profit margin declining to 14.2% due to higher transportation and ramp-up costs, with feed and fuel procurement pressure cited as key distress signals. Company faces external macroeconomic commodity price risks.

Capabilities Needed

Category strategy & demand aggregation for feed commoditiesMulti-geography sourcing & hedging programsSupplier risk analytics & contract optimizationAI-driven commodity price forecasting

Transportation & Logistics Cost Escalation

Container freight and transportation costs contributing to margin pressure. Geopolitical risks (Iran crisis) creating freight cost volatility. Company operates 8,000+ vehicle fleet and imports raw materials globally.

Management cited higher transportation costs as a primary driver of margin decline. Company sources feed from Argentina and US farms, requiring complex international logistics. Container costs and fuel prices directly impact COGS and working capital.

Capabilities Needed

Freight & logistics strategic sourcingRoute optimization & carrier managementFuel hedging & cost containment programsThird-party logistics (3PL) optimization

Supplier Concentration & Geographic Risk

51% supplier concentration in GCC vs 95% in Argentina creates geographic dependency risk. Past supply chain disruptions (2017 diplomatic rift) required rapid rerouting. Working capital and capex intensity higher than global peers.

Heavy reliance on international feed imports from Argentina and US operations exposes company to FX, geopolitical, and supply continuity risks. Limited supplier diversification in key categories.

Capabilities Needed

Supplier diversification strategySupply risk monitoring & early warning systemsAlternative sourcing & dual-sourcing programsSupplier financial health assessment

Where the value lies

Almarai's margin compression (EBIT 14% vs 15% prior year) amid revenue growth presents clear procurement-led value creation opportunity worth $50-100M+ annually. Immediate value in commodity/feed sourcing optimization and transportation cost management addressing direct COGS pressure. Mid-term value from procurement transformation creating sustainable operating model, technology enablement, and capability to support aggressive SAR 18B growth investments while improving cash flow and working capital efficiency.
